manifest.description: Blueprint-compiled draft dispatch pack for phys.mech.hamiltons-equations (Phase 1 front-end; DRAFT only)

assets.title: Hamilton's Equations of Motion ‚ÄĒ core explanation
assets.content:
**Core Insight:** Hamilton's equations are a pair of first-order ODEs that replace Newton's
single second-order ODE. For each generalized coordinate qŠĶĘ with conjugate momentum pŠĶĘ:

    qŐáŠĶĘ = +‚ąāH/‚ąāpŠĶĘ
    ŠĻóŠĶĘ = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ

These 2f equations (f per coordinate, f per momentum) give the equations of motion.
They treat q and p symmetrically. The sign asymmetry (+ for qŐá, ‚ąí for ŠĻó) encodes the
geometry of phase space and is the origin of symplectic structure.

**Properties:**
- H = const along every trajectory (when ‚ąāH/‚ąāt = 0) ‚ÄĒ Liouville's theorem precursor.
- dH/dt = ‚ąāH/‚ąāt (H changes only if explicitly time-dependent).
- Cyclic coordinate qŠĶĘ (‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ = 0) ‚Üí ŠĻóŠĶĘ = 0 ‚Üí pŠĶĘ = const (conservation, same as Lagrangian).

assets.title: Spring-mass
assets.content:
**Worked Example 1 (Spring-mass):**
H = p¬≤/(2m) + ¬Ĺkx¬≤.
qŐá = ‚ąāH/‚ąāp = p/m  ‚Üí  Šļč = p/m  (velocity from momentum)
ŠĻó = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāx = ‚ąíkx  ‚Üí  dp/dt = ‚ąíkx  (Newton's law: F = ‚ąíkx)

Combining: Šļć = ŠĻó/m = ‚ąíkx/m  ‚Üí  Šļć + (k/m)x = 0 ‚úď

assets.title: Pendulum
assets.content:
**Worked Example 2 (Pendulum):**
H = p_őł¬≤/(2mL¬≤) ‚ąí mgL cosőł.
őłŐá = ‚ąāH/‚ąāp_őł = p_őł/(mL¬≤)
ŠĻó_őł = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāőł = ‚ąímgL sinőł

Combining: mL¬≤őłŐą = ŠĻó_őł = ‚ąímgL sinőł  ‚Üí  őłŐą + (g/L) sinőł = 0 ‚úď

assets.title: Central force, angular momentum
assets.content:
**Worked Example 3 (Central force, angular momentum):**
H = p_r¬≤/(2m) + p_ŌÜ¬≤/(2mr¬≤) + V(r).
ŌÜŐá = ‚ąāH/‚ąāp_ŌÜ = p_ŌÜ/(mr¬≤);  ŠĻó_ŌÜ = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāŌÜ = 0  ‚Üí  p_ŌÜ = const (angular momentum).
rŐą: combine rŐą = ‚ąāH/‚ąāp_r|_time derivative ‚Üí gives effective radial equation.

---

assets.title: Priority 1
assets.content:
### MC-SIGN-BOTH-POSITIVE (Priority 1)
**Label:** "Both Hamilton's equations have + signs: qŐá = +‚ąāH/‚ąāp and ŠĻó = +‚ąāH/‚ąāq"

**Trigger signals:**
- Writes ŠĻóŠĶĘ = +‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ (misses the minus sign)
- Gets wrong sign for force in spring problem
- Says "the minus sign looks wrong ‚ÄĒ shouldn't force be in the direction of increasing q?"

**Conflict evidence [P28]:**
"Spring: H = p¬≤/(2m) + ¬Ĺkx¬≤. If ŠĻó = +‚ąāH/‚ąāx = +kx, then dp/dt = +kx ‚Üí force is in +x
direction when stretched to +x. But a compressed/stretched spring exerts restoring force:
F = ‚ąíkx (toward equilibrium). The + sign gives the wrong physics. Where does the ‚ąí come from?"

**Bridge [P30]:**
"The ‚ąí sign in ŠĻó = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ comes from the Legendre transform. In Lagrangian terms:
ŠĻóŠĶĘ = d/dt(‚ąāL/‚ąāqŐáŠĶĘ) = ‚ąāL/‚ąāqŠĶĘ (E-L equation). And ‚ąāL/‚ąāq = ‚ąí‚ąāV/‚ąāq = F (since L = T ‚ąí V).
So ŠĻóŠĶĘ = ‚ąāL/‚ąāqŠĶĘ. But H = ő£pqŐá ‚ąí L ‚Üí ‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ = ‚ąí‚ąāL/‚ąāqŠĶĘ. Hence ŠĻóŠĶĘ = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ.
The minus sign is forced by the Legendre transform."

**Replacement [P31]:**
"Memorize the asymmetric pair: qŐáŠĶĘ = +‚ąāH/‚ąāpŠĶĘ and ŠĻóŠĶĘ = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ. Plus for q, minus for p.
Mnemonic: 'q flows with H's momentum gradient; p flows against H's position gradient.'"

**Discrimination pairs [P33]:**
- Spring with ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāx = ‚ąíkx: restoring force (correct) ‚úď
- Spring with +‚ąāH/‚ąāx = +kx: anti-restoring force (wrong physics) ‚úó

**s6_path:** "Write the equations at the top of every problem: qŐá = +‚ąāH/‚ąāp, ŠĻó = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāq.
Box them. Refer back every time."

---

assets.title: Priority 2
assets.content:
### MC-TWO-EQUATIONS-REDUNDANT (Priority 2)
**Label:** "Hamilton's two equations are just two ways of writing the same equation"

**Trigger signals:**
- Derives only one of the two equations and stops
- Claims Šļč = p/m is trivial (just the definition of momentum)
- Does not see that BOTH are needed to get the full dynamics

**Conflict evidence [P28]:**
"For the spring: if I only use ŠĻó = ‚ąíkx, I have dp/dt = ‚ąíkx. One equation in two unknowns
(x and p). I need a second equation to solve for both. The second equation Šļč = p/m tells me
how x and p are related dynamically. Together they give Šļć = ‚ąí(k/m)x. Without both, I'm stuck.
Why is Šļč = p/m not 'trivially obvious'?"

**Bridge [P30]:**
"In phase space, x and p are independent variables. Šļč = p/m is the equation of motion for x
(how x evolves in time); ŠĻó = ‚ąíkx is the equation of motion for p. Both are needed to integrate
the trajectory (x(t), p(t)) in phase space. In Hamiltonian mechanics, we don't have Šļć ‚ÄĒ only
first-order equations Šļč and ŠĻó, which together give second-order dynamics."

**Replacement [P31]:**
"Hamilton's equations: always write BOTH. qŐá = +‚ąāH/‚ąāp gives the velocity equation.
ŠĻó = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāq gives the force equation. Together they fully determine (q(t), p(t))."

**Discrimination pairs [P33]:**
- Using only ŠĻó = ‚ąíkx: stuck with two unknowns (x, p) and one equation ‚úď
- Using both: solve to get Šļć + (k/m)x = 0, fully determined ‚úď

**s6_path:** "Two equations per DOF, always. Šļč equation (velocity) + ŠĻó equation (force)."

---

assets.title: Apply ‚ÄĒ spring Hamilton's equations
assets.content:
**MP-1 (Apply ‚ÄĒ spring Hamilton's equations):**
"H = p¬≤/(2m) + ¬Ĺkx¬≤. Write both Hamilton's equations and combine them to get Šļć + (k/m)x = 0."
Expected: Šļč = p/m; ŠĻó = ‚ąíkx; differentiate first: Šļć = ŠĻó/m = ‚ąíkx/m ‚úď.
_Discriminates: both equations; correct signs; combination procedure._

assets.title: Apply ‚ÄĒ pendulum equations
assets.content:
**MP-2 (Apply ‚ÄĒ pendulum equations):**
"H = p_őł¬≤/(2mL¬≤) ‚ąí mgL cosőł. Write Hamilton's equations and derive őłŐą + (g/L) sinőł = 0."
Expected: őłŐá = p_őł/(mL¬≤); ŠĻó_őł = ‚ąímgL sinőł; combine: mL¬≤őłŐą = ‚ąímgL sinőł ‚úď.
_Discriminates: pendulum H; sign of ŠĻó_őł._

assets.title: Apply ‚ÄĒ conservation from Hamilton
assets.content:
**MP-3 (Apply ‚ÄĒ conservation from Hamilton):**
"H = p_r¬≤/(2m) + p_ŌÜ¬≤/(2mr¬≤) + V(r). Show that angular momentum is conserved using
Hamilton's equations (without solving for the trajectory)."
Expected: ŠĻó_ŌÜ = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāŌÜ = 0 ‚Üí p_ŌÜ = const.
_Discriminates: conservation from cyclic coordinate in H._

assets.title: Apply ‚ÄĒ energy conservation
assets.content:
**MP-4 (Apply ‚ÄĒ energy conservation):**
"Show that dH/dt = ‚ąāH/‚ąāt for any H(q, p, t) using Hamilton's equations."
Expected: dH/dt = ő£ŠĶĘ[(‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ)qŐáŠĶĘ + (‚ąāH/‚ąāpŠĶĘ)ŠĻóŠĶĘ] + ‚ąāH/‚ąāt
= ő£ŠĶĘ[(‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ)(‚ąāH/‚ąāpŠĶĘ) + (‚ąāH/‚ąāpŠĶĘ)(‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ)] + ‚ąāH/‚ąāt = 0 + ‚ąāH/‚ąāt = ‚ąāH/‚ąāt.
_Discriminates: chain rule + Hamilton's equations; algebraic cancellation._

assets.title: Apply ‚ÄĒ 3D central force
assets.content:
**MP-5 (Apply ‚ÄĒ 3D central force):**
"H = p_r¬≤/(2m) + p_őł¬≤/(2mr¬≤) + p_ŌÜ¬≤/(2mr¬≤ sin¬≤őł) + V(r) (spherical coordinates).
Write ŠĻó_ŌÜ and ŠĻó_őł. What is conserved? Why is p_őł not conserved?"
Expected: ŠĻó_ŌÜ = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāŌÜ = 0 ‚Üí p_ŌÜ = L_z = const.
ŠĻó_őł = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāőł = (p_ŌÜ¬≤ cosőł)/(mr¬≤ sin¬≥őł) ‚Č† 0 ‚Üí p_őł not conserved (őł appears in H).
_Discriminates: 3D spherical Hamilton's equations; identifying which momenta are constant._

---

assets.title: Prerequisite Dependency Map
assets.content:
```
phys.mech.hamiltonian  ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ  phys.mech.hamiltons-equations
```

**PD-1 (Hamiltonian construction):**
"Derive H for a simple pendulum (L = ¬ĹmL¬≤őłŐá¬≤ + mgL cosőł)."
Expected: H = p_őł¬≤/(2mL¬≤) ‚ąí mgL cosőł.

**PD-2 (Partial derivatives of H):**
"For H = p¬≤/(2m) + ¬Ĺkx¬≤, compute ‚ąāH/‚ąāp and ‚ąāH/‚ąāx."
Expected: ‚ąāH/‚ąāp = p/m; ‚ąāH/‚ąāx = kx.

Both must pass before TA-3.

---

assets.title: Formative Assessment Strategy
assets.content:
**FA-1 (TA-2 exit):** "Write both Hamilton's equations from memory. Which has + and which has ‚ąí?"
Threshold: qŐáŠĶĘ = +‚ąāH/‚ąāpŠĶĘ; ŠĻóŠĶĘ = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāqŠĶĘ.

**FA-2 (TA-4 exit):** "Pendulum: correct sign for ŠĻó_őł?"
Expected: ŠĻó_őł = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāőł = ‚ąímgL sinőł. If student writes +mgL sinőł ‚Üí MC-SIGN-BOTH-POSITIVE not resolved.

**FA-3 (TA-5 exit):** "Why do you need BOTH Hamilton's equations, not just ŠĻó = ‚ąí‚ąāH/‚ąāq?"
Expected: Need both to update (q, p) in phase space; Šļč = p/m tells x how to evolve given p;
without it, cannot integrate trajectory.

**FA-4 (Session exit):** MP-1 and MP-2. Both correct ‚Üí advance to TA-7.

---

assets.title: Anxiety & Confidence Protocols
assets.content:
**S6 (Anxiety) path:**
- Stay on spring-mass and pendulum only.
- Emphasize: two partial derivatives, two equations. No second derivatives needed.
- Skip phase space topology (TA-5 problem 3 ‚ÄĒ inverted oscillator).

**S7 (Overconfident) path [Protocol G]:**
- MP-4 (algebraic proof of dH/dt = ‚ąāH/‚ąāt).
- MP-5 (3D spherical Hamilton's equations; őł momentum not conserved).
- Preview: Poisson brackets as the next natural step.

**S4 (Prereq gap):**
- PD-1 gap (Hamiltonian construction) ‚Üí return to Legendre transform; cannot apply Hamilton's
  equations without knowing H.

---

assets.title: Spaced Retrieval & Interleaving Plan
assets.content:
**Session 1:** TA-1 through TA-4 (Concrete ‚Üí equations statement ‚Üí MC-SIGN probe).
**Session 2 (‚Č•24 h later):** Retrieval: "Write Hamilton's equations from memory for H = p¬≤/2m + ¬Ĺkx¬≤."
  ‚Üí TA-5 (MC-REDUNDANT probe) ‚Üí TA-6 problems 1‚Äď3.
**Session 3 (‚Č•72 h later):** "For the Kepler problem H = p_r¬≤/(2m) + p_ŌÜ¬≤/(2mr¬≤) ‚ąí k/r,
  write all four Hamilton's equations and identify what is conserved."
**Pre-Poisson-brackets session:** All 5 P91 probes cold; require 4/5.

**Interleaving partners:** phys.mech.hamiltonian (prerequisite),
phys.mech.poisson-brackets (next concept),
phys.mech.cyclic-coordinates-conservation-laws (conservation in H formulation).

---
